mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.94% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation above 4.5% signals ongoing tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces near-term corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 135,106 thousand shows labor market stabilization. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100 thousand signals strengthening labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 100 thousand in a single month
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases > 100k are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 364,198 million dollars combined with housing starts at 1,849 thousand indicate consumer and construction demand weakness. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, this signals demand contraction that reduces equity valuations and forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 364,198M and HOUST at 1,849k as of 2011-10/11
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 112.0991 (2017=100) reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced production capacity that constrains earnings growth and signals economic deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.6% combined with elevated federal funds rate at 4.94% creates a regime where labor market tightness meets monetary restriction. When UNRATE falls below 4.8% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, the Fed faces pressure to maintain restrictive policy despite labor strength, signaling policy conflict that increases equity risk premium and produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of 2011-11
regime_hint: monetary tightening with tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint condition of tight labor market and high rates fails to predict negative returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 201.9 (1982-1984=100) indicates cumulative price pressures. When month-over-month CPI growth exceeds 0.3%, the Fed maintains restrictive policy stance that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure regime with monetary tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following CPI increases > 0.3% are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 11,394.7 billion (chained 2017 dollars) combined with housing starts at 1,849 thousand indicates demand-side momentum. When GDPC1 growth accelerates quarter-over-quarter while HOUST rises month-over-month, this signals robust demand expansion that supports equity earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth accelerates and HOUST rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,394.7B and HOUST at 1,849k as of 2011-10
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in GDP growth and housing starts fails to predict positive returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 112.0991 combined with payroll employment at 135,106 thousand indicates supply-side capacity constraints. When INDPRO rises while PAYEMS growth slows below 50 thousand per month, this signals productivity gains without labor expansion that supports margin expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while PAYEMS monthly increase is below 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.0991 and PAYEMS at 135,106k as of 2011-11
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with labor efficiency gains
failure_condition: Joint condition of rising production with slowing employment fails to predict positive returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.
